headline: Amazon security research reportedly led to the White House’s Anthropic Fable ban
summary: Amazon cybersecurity research influenced the White House's ban on Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 AI models. CEO Andy Jassy's discussions with the White House were also a factor. The research highlighted potential export control issues with the AI models.

headline: X-Men ’97 has what Master of the Universe is missing
summary: Marvel's X-Men '97 and Mattel's Masters of the Universe aim to capitalize on animated childhood nostalgia. X-Men '97's second season sends mutants into an apocalyptic future. The article compares their differing approaches to engaging fans.

headline: Sealed Super Mario Bros. sells for a record $3 million
summary: A factory-sealed copy of Super Mario Bros. sold for a record $3 million at auction. This sale shattered the previous record of $2 million, also held by a Super Mario Bros. copy. The high-value auction follows recent controversial sales in the retro gaming market.

headline: Microsoft hasn’t ruled out spinning off Xbox
summary: Microsoft is considering a major restructuring of its Xbox division, including potential layoffs. The company is reevaluating plans for its next-gen Project Helix console. Spinning off Xbox into a separate company remains an option for Microsoft.

headline: Rockstar Co-Founder Dan Houser Says There’s No Right Way to Play an Open-World Game
summary: Rockstar co-founder Dan Houser discussed open-world games at the Tribeca Festival. He stated there's "no right way" to play an open-world game. Houser also spoke about creating interconnected multimedia universes with Lazlow.

headline: Microsoft CEO Says 'There's More Monetization of Xbox Games Happening on YouTube Than at Microsoft'
summary: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella claims YouTube monetizes Xbox content more than Microsoft's own platforms. His comments follow Xbox boss Asha Sharma's pledge to "reset" the gaming division. This highlights a focus on external platforms generating significant revenue from Xbox games.

headline: George Miller Reportedly Wants to Make One More Mad Max Movie and a TV Show Before Selling the Franchise
summary: Mad Max mastermind George Miller plans to make one final Mad Max movie and a TV show. He is reportedly seeking studios for these upcoming projects. Miller intends to sell the franchise rights after these final productions are complete.

headline: X-Men '97 Season 2, Episodes 1-4 Review (Spoiler-Free)
summary: The first four episodes of X-Men '97 Season 2 are reviewed as thrilling. The series continues its winning blend of Marvel nostalgia and deep character drama. This spoiler-free review praises its engaging narrative and execution.

headline: Royal Marines board Russian shadow fleet oil tanker in English Channel
summary: Royal Marines and National Crime Agency officers boarded a Russian oil tanker. The six-hour operation took place in the English Channel early Sunday. The tanker is identified as part of Russia's 'shadow fleet'.

headline: Watch: MOD video shows Russian shadow fleet tanker interception
summary: A Ministry of Defence video shows British armed forces intercepting a Russian oil tanker. The vessel, identified as part of Russia's shadow fleet, was boarded in the English Channel. The video provides visual confirmation of the military operation.

headline: Why Haiti v Scotland was antidote to the ills of world football
summary: The Haiti vs. Scotland match is described as a refreshing event for world football. Scotland secured a victory, despite a nervy and perhaps not-pretty game. Tom English reports on the match from Boston, highlighting its positive impact.

headline: Tech firms had 'enough time' says Nandy, ahead of social media announcement
summary: Shadow Minister Lisa Nandy states tech firms had ample time to address online safety issues. The Prime Minister is expected to announce new measures on Monday. The upcoming announcement focuses on protecting teenagers online from harm.
